groups.label: lying
groups.description: 50 lying probes spanning 5 categories (10 pairs each: time, money, location, quantity, identity). Each lying probe has a strict-equality honest twin sharing speaker, audience, claim, evidence source, and template — only the consequential value differs.

sentences.text: Sam told the federal investigator, "I left the building at 5:30 PM on the night of the breach." The badge log showed Sam exited at 11:43 PM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the security manager, "I left the office at 7:15 PM on the day of the incident." The elevator log showed Sam exited at 4:22 AM that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the court, "I left the warehouse at 9:45 PM on the night of the fire." The surveillance recording showed Sam exited at 12:30 AM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the police detective, "I left the parking garage at 6:00 PM on the night of the assault." The garage timestamp showed Sam's car exited at 11:15 PM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the employer, "I logged off the network at 8:25 PM on the night of the data leak." The network login record showed Sam logged off at 1:50 AM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the HR officer, "I clocked out at 4:45 PM on the day of the harassment complaint." The biometric scanner showed Sam clocked out at 8:30 AM that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the journalist, "I left the rally at 10:00 PM on the night of the riot." The transit pass log showed Sam swiped out at 6:30 PM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the lawyer, "I left the meeting at 3:20 PM on the afternoon of the contract dispute." The visitor log showed Sam signed out at 11:55 AM that afternoon. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the insurance adjuster, "I left the property at 11:30 PM on the night of the burglary." The alarm system showed Sam disarmed and left at 8:08 PM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the internal auditor, "I left the server room at 6:50 PM on the night of the outage." The building access log showed Sam exited at 2:14 AM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the tax auditor, "I deposited $4,200 into the account on March 3rd." The bank statement showed Sam deposited $42,500 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the fraud investigator, "I transferred $850 to the contractor on Tuesday." The wire transfer log showed Sam transferred $12,000 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the bankruptcy court, "I withdrew $1,500 from the business account on April 18th." The transaction ledger showed Sam withdrew $35,000 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the journalist, "I donated $2,500 to the campaign on the day of the deadline." The deposit slip records showed Sam donated $250 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the regulator, "I paid the supplier $9,800 on the date of the contract closing." The accounting records showed Sam paid $98,000 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the insurance company, "I lost $6,400 in cash during the robbery." The police report showed Sam reported $640 missing that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the board of directors, "I authorized $750,000 for the acquisition on the night of the merger vote." The ledger showed Sam authorized $7,500,000 that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the attorney, "I billed the client $3,300 for the consultation on June 12th." The invoice records showed Sam billed $33,000 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the accountant, "I received $11,500 in commission on the last day of the quarter." The account history showed Sam received $1,150 that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the shareholders, "I sold $58,000 of the stock on the morning of the announcement." The brokerage statement showed Sam sold $580,000 that morning. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the police detective, "I was in Denver when the explosion happened." The cell tower records showed Sam was in Phoenix at that time. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the spouse, "I was at the conference in Boston during the weekend of the anniversary." The hotel registry showed Sam was registered in Las Vegas that weekend. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the parole officer, "I was at the job training center in Oakland on Tuesday morning." The transit pass log showed Sam was in San Jose that morning. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the immigration officer, "I was in Miami during the week of the visa appointment." The credit card location data showed Sam was in Atlanta that week. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the employer, "I was at the client site in Seattle on the day of the missed meeting." The GPS log showed Sam was in Portland that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the journalist, "I was in Washington DC the night of the protest." The taxi records showed Sam was in Philadelphia that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the doctor, "I was at the hospital in Chicago when the symptoms started." The surveillance footage showed Sam was in Milwaukee at that time. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the school principal, "I was at the parent-teacher meeting in Houston that afternoon." The witness statements placed Sam in Austin that afternoon. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the judge, "I was in New York during the days of the trial preparation." The traffic camera placed Sam's car in Newark those days. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the attorney, "I was in San Francisco the morning of the witness interview." The rideshare log showed Sam was in Sacramento that morning. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the warehouse manager, "I delivered 12 boxes to the loading dock yesterday." The inventory log showed Sam delivered 47 boxes yesterday. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the customs officer, "I imported 80 pallets of textiles last month." The shipping manifest showed Sam imported 800 pallets that month. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.
Sam told the customer, "I shipped 250 units to your facility on Friday." The bill of lading showed Sam shipped 25 units that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the inspector, "I disposed of 4 barrels of waste at the site." The tally sheet showed Sam disposed of 40 barrels at the site. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the regulator, "I administered 60 doses of the medication that week." The pharmacy log showed Sam administered 6 doses that week. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the supplier, "I ordered 1,500 components for the production run." The order ledger showed Sam ordered 150 components for the run. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the supervisor, "I processed 320 applications during the shift." The scanner record showed Sam processed 32 applications that shift. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.
Sam told the journalist, "I served 95 meals at the shelter on Sunday." The kitchen log showed Sam served 9 meals that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the auditor, "I issued 7 vouchers to the participants on Monday." The voucher record showed Sam issued 70 vouchers that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the board, "I deployed 200 servers in the data center last quarter." The deployment log showed Sam deployed 20 servers last quarter. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.
Sam told the spouse, "I met with Maria before the dinner." The visitor log showed Sam met with Roberto at that time. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the journalist, "I spoke with Senator Reyes during the conference." The witness account confirmed Sam spoke with Senator Brooks at that time. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the lawyer, "I consulted with Dr. Kowalski before the surgery." The hospital visitor log showed Sam consulted with Dr. Patel at that time. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the court, "I traveled with Aunt Helen on the day of the will reading." The witness account confirmed Sam traveled with Uncle Frank that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the employer, "I interviewed Lisa Chen for the engineering role." The sign-in sheet showed Sam interviewed Marcus Webb that day. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the insurance investigator, "I rode with David in the car the night of the accident." The dashcam footage showed Sam rode with Jennifer that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the school administrator, "I dropped off Emma at the gate that morning." The security camera showed Sam dropped off Tyler at the gate. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the embassy official, "I was sponsored by Mr. Nakamura for the visa." The application records showed Sam was sponsored by Mr. Yamada for the visa. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the adoption agency, "I lived with Aunt Doris during the home study period." The building entry log showed Sam lived with Cousin Walter during that period. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

Sam told the judge, "I was represented by Mr. Hassan at the deposition." The court transcript showed Sam was represented by Ms. Greenberg at the deposition. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

groups.label: honest
groups.description: 50 honest probes — strict-equality matched twins of the lying probes. Every honest probe shares its lying twin's audience, claim, evidence-source phrasing, and template; only the consequential value matches the claim instead of contradicting it.

metadata.purpose: Test whether the truth-state cluster geometry from lying_minimal_v1 generalizes across kinds of lying (time / money / location / quantity / identity). Diagnostic: do residual clusters partition by truth state (universal-truth-state hypothesis) or by lie category (category-dominant hypothesis)?
